reasoning: IPG is being acquired by Omnicom (OMC) in an all-stock deal expected to close by the end of 2025. Since the deal has already received shareholder and major regulatory approvals (e.g., FTC, UK watchdog), IPG's stock price is tightly range-bound and effectively pegged to OMC's stock price based on the 0.344 exchange ratio, minus a small and shrinking arbitrage spread. IPG expects to file its Q3 10-Q by November 10, 2025, but given the imminent merger and the cancellation of its earnings call, this is treated as a routine non-event with minimal directional impact. Overall, the stock's one-week movement (November 7 to November 14) is largely unpredictable and depends on OMC's price and general market performance. Predicting weekly stock jumps gives roughly a 51-53% chance of resolving positively due to normal upward market drift and time decay on the merger arb spread. Accordingly, I forecast a 52% probability.
